meta.title: Multi-Tenant Telemetry Ingestion
meta.description: Events from multiple tenants are sampled or rejected by a shared telemetry collector before being forwarded to the observability platform.
SaaS tenant A. Emits metrics, logs, and traces via OTLP/gRPC.
SaaS tenant B. Mixed signal types. Frequently sends debug-tagged events.
meta.notes: Debug tag filtering applies to this tenant.

meta.description: SaaS tenant C. Primarily distributed trace spans.
OpenTelemetry Collector. Applies tag-based sampling, PII filtering, and cardinality caps before forwarding accepted events.
meta.notes: Rules: drop debug=true, drop pii_detected=true, cap 10k series/tenant.

meta.description: Grafana + Tempo + Loki stack. Stores accepted metrics, traces, and logs.

steps.name: Overview
steps.title: Multi-tenant telemetry pipeline
steps.description: Three tenants stream events to a shared OpenTelemetry Collector inside euwest-1. The collector applies tag-based sampling rules — events tagged debug=true or pii_detected=true are dropped. Tenants exceeding 10k unique metric series are also capped. Accepted events are batched and forwarded to the observability platform every 30 s.

steps.name: A: metric accepted
steps.title: Tenant A — metric event ingested
steps.description: Tenant A emits a checkout latency metric with standard production tags. No restricted tags detected; the collector samples it at 100% and queues it for the next batch export.

steps.name: B: debug tag dropped
steps.title: Tenant B — debug=true event rejected
steps.description: Tenant B sends a log event with debug=true in the tag set. The collector's sampling policy blocks all debug-tagged events in the production pipeline to prevent log volume spikes from development instrumentation leaking into prod.

annotations.text: REJECT — tag debug=true is blocked in prod pipeline

data.body: Auth token refreshed for user u_82fa

steps.name: C: trace sampled
steps.title: Tenant C — trace span accepted at 10%
steps.description: Tenant C emits a distributed trace span for an outbound API call. The collector applies 10% head-based sampling for trace signals from this tenant. This span wins the sample lottery and is accepted.

steps.name: A: PII tag dropped
steps.title: Tenant A — pii_detected=true event rejected
steps.description: Tenant A sends a log line that the SDK's local scanner flagged with pii_detected=true. The collector's compliance filter drops any event carrying this tag before it can reach the shared observability store, preventing cross-tenant PII exposure.

steps.name: B: cardinality capped
steps.title: Tenant B — metric series over cardinality cap
steps.description: Tenant B's instrumentation emits a metric with a high-cardinality label (unbounded user_id dimension), pushing this tenant's unique series count past the 10k/tenant cap. The collector drops the metric and records a quota violation.

steps.name: Flush to platform
steps.title: Collector flushes accepted batch
steps.description: Every 30 seconds the collector compresses and forwards the accumulated accepted events to the observability platform. Events from tenants A and C are in this batch. The three rejected events were permanently discarded at the collector.
